name: A.B.D.
description_short: The American Board of Dermatology is a non-profit medical specialty board that certifies dermatologists and maintains professional standards—not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
The American Board of Dermatology (ABD) is one of 24 medical specialty boards that comprise the American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS). Founded by the dermatology profession itself, ABD operates as an independent, non-profit organization with a mission to serve the public by certifying that dermatologists meet high standards of knowledge, experience, and lifelong learning. The organization was established to protect consumers by assessing and certifying physicians who meet specific educational, training, and professional requirements.

ABD's primary services include board certification exams and continuing certification programs for dermatologists. They administer multiple certification pathways including the BASIC exam for first-year residents, the CORE exam (offered three times yearly), the APPLIED exam, and subspecialty certifications in Dermatopathology, Pediatric Dermatology, and Micrographic Dermatologic Surgery (MDS). The organization also maintains a public directory allowing patients to verify whether their dermatologist is board-certified, and offers continuing education resources to help certified dermatologists stay current with advances in diagnoses and treatment.

ABD distinguishes itself through peer-established standards that reflect specialty-specific expertise rather than generic credentialing. Their continuing certification program has been streamlined as of January 1, 2026, to simplify compliance while maintaining rigor. Additionally, ABD participates in the ABMS Scholars Program, offering $15,000 awards to support research and professional development among early-career physicians, junior faculty, fellows, and residents. The organization provides educational materials in multiple languages to help patients understand the value of board certification.

ABD is fundamentally a professional credentialing and quality assurance organization, not a consumer finance service. It provides no financial products, loans, credit services, or money-related assistance. Any categorization as a payday-alternative or other consumer finance category is factually incorrect. The organization's value to consumers lies primarily in the ability to verify dermatologist credentials, not in financial aid or lending.
services: BASIC Exam certification for first-year dermatology residents
CORE Exam certification with multiple annual administrations (Winter, Summer, Fall)
APPLIED Exam certification for dermatologists seeking additional validation
Micrographic Dermatologic Surgery (MDS) Subspecialty Certification Exam
Continuing Certification Program with streamlined requirements effective 2026
Public directory search tool to verify board-certified dermatologists
Educational materials and flyers (English and Spanish) explaining board certification
ABMS Scholars Program supporting research and professional development
Professional standards development and peer-established competency frameworks
pros: Operates as an independent, non-profit organization with no financial conflicts of interest
Maintains a free public search tool to verify board-certified dermatologists by name, first name, or NPI
Offers multiple certification pathways (BASIC, CORE, APPLIED) reflecting different career stages
Provides subspecialty certification in Dermatopathology, Pediatric Dermatology, and Micrographic Surgery
Streamlined continuing certification program as of 2026 reduces administrative burden while maintaining standards
Supports early-career research through ABMS Scholars Program with $15,000 awards
Supplies educational materials in English and Spanish to help patients identify certified dermatologists
cons: Does not provide any consumer financial services, loans, or credit assistance despite current miscategorization
Certification is optional and not all dermatologists choose to pursue or maintain board certification
Public directory search tool requires dermatologist name or NPI—limited by user knowledge of provider details
Organization serves professionals (dermatologists), not consumers seeking financial or medical services directly
No direct consumer support or patient-facing financial counseling available
best_for: Patients seeking to verify whether their dermatologist holds current board certification
Medical professionals in dermatology pursuing specialty board certification and continuing education
Employers or healthcare organizations vetting dermatologist credentials for credentialing purposes
diagnosis: ABD is a professional credentialing organization for dermatologists, not a consumer finance service. It should not appear on a consumer finance platform. The company is best used by patients needing to verify dermatologist credentials and by medical professionals pursuing board certification—not by consumers seeking financial products or services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
